hypothesis: Incorporating real-time commodity price forecasting models (e.g., time-series ML models trained on historical raw material price volatility and geopolitical risk indices) directly into the analysis module's economic efficiency metric will reveal that synthesis route rankings for Ca2SiO4 and analogous refractory silicates undergo statistically significant inversions at predictable price-ratio thresholds between abundant precursors (CaO, SiO2) and higher-purity alternatives (Ca3SiO5, Si3N4), such that a dynamic optimization workflow incorporating these thresholds will identify procurement timing strategies that reduce average synthesis cost by more than 30% compared to static price-based route selection over a multi-year production horizon.
